scene.description: A fog-covered shoreline. A lighthouse stands at the center — steady, certain, and impossible. The records say it was dismantled decades before this memory.

fragments.label: The Lighthouse — Structure
fragments.text: You study its structure. Stone, weathered, real. Too real for something that was never here.

fragments.label: The Turning Light
fragments.text: You watch the light turn. It sweeps the fog but lands on nothing. There is no one to warn.

fragments.label: The Shoreline
fragments.text: You look at the shore around it. The coastline doesn't match any recorded geography.

fragments.label: The Distance
fragments.text: You measure the distance. Each time you look away and back, it is slightly nearer.

fragments.label: The Keeper's Name
fragments.text: The subject's file says they were alone. But there are two sets of footprints in the wet sand, and one set is yours.

fragments.label: The Lighthouse Door
fragments.text: The door at its base is ajar. Inside: a desk identical to yours at the Bureau. You have never been here. You know exactly where the third drawer sticks.

fragments.label: The Foghorn
fragments.text: The foghorn sounds — but it's a recording of a voice, slowed down past recognition. When you speed it up in your head, it is saying your name.

interpretations.label: Rule it a misclassified historical record
Rule it interpretation drift — the memory is true, the meaning slid
